Our tentacle legs are far too slow to evade jungle predators — even vanae have a hard time leaving a fight unscathed with their stupid, sexy tree-legs,” she says before taking another mouthful. “Are we even then?” The hungry gal turns her sightless eyes on you. “This time you are good, [flags.PAULETTE_MHENGA_DATE |but don’t think you’ll get away with food over and over again |but you better ask for my permission to pick my little water babies the next time you come ]. I think I’ll need some time to recover from the experience, sort the pictures my visor took, and take care of our findings,” she says, running her clawed hand on the cargobot’s containment shells. “I’ll see you on board captain, don’t make us wait too long[silly |~ |. ]” The alien duo waves at you then head toward the city proper, their overloaded cargobot in tow. original: “Be my guests,” you reply, offering your arm to the former XO. The blue leyak swirls with glee before perching himself on Paulette’s armored shoulder. Without a word, she wraps her left arm around yours and lets you guide them through the gold myrs’ capital. It takes you some minutes to stroll through the caverns, admiring the big mushrooms and the exotic growths that compose this place’s stunning flora as you come closer and closer to the dynamic streets of the merchant district. Soon, the vegetation becomes less prominent around you, replaced by an array of buildings not unlike the ones from ancient Terra’s pre-space exploration era — buildings sometimes pockmarked by the apparent scars of war. Here and there, you notice some rubbles left to clear, craters freckling the paved roads, or scaffolds hugging injured buildings while the locals fix them the best they can in the hope that the cease fire will turn into a lasting peace. Your crewmates contemplate this uncanny spectacle with wide, enchanted eyes, entranced by how lively and active this place is in spite of how close the frontlines are. You have to stop from time to time as the two of them stay gawking for long, silent moments at a particular spot then resume their trek through the crowded district again. Your companion’s visor flickers whenever you pass near an interesting piece of architecture that survived the bombardments, small memories of a place that might disappear if peace isn’t achieved soon. People give the three of you weird, pensive glances as you cross the streets, pondering if what they see is real or a vivid dream — you notice even other offworlders puzzled by your presence. “This place reminds me of Dhaal a bit, minus the civil war of course. Lots of beautiful things scattered around, but with rough edges here and there that show the deep cracks hidden under the fancy facade.” “[var0 |Isn’t the zaibatsu rivalry akin to the myr war |Don’t you have conflicts on your homeworld ]?” Paulette nods, gazing at her sleek prosthetics. “Yes and no. The three zaibatsu controlling Dhaal are always fighting for dominance but ultimately, they are codependent. The XO process was designed by their common effort to better control the working classes and choke out any attempt to organize counter powers. Here, it looks more like a full-fledged war that ravaged both sides. I heard from the red ambassadors’ gossip that they lost so many males on both sides, their species were in danger of extinction before the U.G.C showed up and forced them to see a way bigger picture.” “Yeah, they’ll have to adapt. If I may ask, which side would you back if you had to choose?” you ask as you pass next to a military checkpoint. “I’d rather not be involved in their war. When we asked for accreditation, the gold and red agents tried to recruit us as mercenaries. This world is far from peaceful but I’d rather keep it far from war instead — I don’t trust any of them to solve their respective people’s problems. The reds’ society was built by and for conflict; I doubt they’ll fare well in times of peace. Especially if they have to lead a society incorporating the golds after all they’ve gone through. As for the golds, look how quick their society became belligerent and authoritarian as soon as they were in trouble, lightyears away from their shining ideals. I just hope that whatever happens, they won’t achieve peace via mutual destruction. This planet has a lot more to offer than hatred and greed,” she says. Mustering his courage and overcoming his shyness, Dabbebieth bounces on the cyborg’s arm and sets his eyes on you. “They must be like ganraels — neither soft nor hard, solid nor liquid — they must adapt as individuals and as a society.” It takes you a few seconds to understand what happened, leaving enough time for the poetic leyak to hop back onto Paulette’s shoulder and turn his attention to a nearby source of music. Shaking the confusion away, you slowly approach the gold myr and vulkrim couple playing a beautiful duet together. The latter’s air sounds familiar to you, a melancholic whine played with a wooden flute while the native plays a more nostalgic tune with her own metallic pipe. There’s a magnificent harmony between their instruments, complementing each other rather than fighting for dominance. One tune feels like finally reaching the end of a fruitful journey while the other reads more like returning home after a tedious ordeal — then they meet and cross the door, together at long last. When the music ends, you turn around, noticing the state of your music-ravished poet and a misty-eyed Paulette. “[pc.kmhbb |Our resident artist wasn’t the most touched of the bunch, |I see you choose to be like a ganrael today, |Not so tough and serious anymore, are you? |Daww, you wanted to hear more, didn’t you? |It hits you in the feels too? ]” you tease, flustering the purple cutie and making the shy leyak laugh merrily. “No, I’ve got something in my eyes.” “What’s blocking your sight then?” “Tears!” The emotive zaika wipes her brow carefully, inhaling deep breaths to calm her racing heart while the assembled spectators tip the players with gemstones and credit chips. In the blink of an eye, Dabbebieth’s tentacles recover a small shard from Paulette’s satchel and drop it in the hat the couple uses to collect their wage to the breathless myr’s delight. After a long minute of hyperventilating, the violet cyborg tightens her grip on your arm and lets you guide her through the city again, still a bit concussed by the whirlwind of emotions that rocked her to her core. She stays quiet for long minutes, leaving the blue leyak free to enjoy and feel their way through Gildenmere’s warmth and cultural melting pot as you cross stone arteries boiling with life. Small restaurants and shops attract a lot of visitors and locals, a curtain of normalcy shrouding the cruel fate that awaits this place if anyone revokes the cease fire. Life continues on despite everything that’s happened, slowly healing the gruesome wounds the conflict inflicted to this planet’s civilisations and people. Maybe with the U.G.C’s active help and a good amount of pride and self-preservation, the myrmedions will spread their hopes and dreams across the stars. [flags.PAULETTE_MYR_DATE |As your date comes to an end, you take a last stop at Silly Slimes, Amaranthe’s shop. You find the orange myr at the same spot she was on your first visit, but this time filling her empty shelves with colorful toys. “Hey! If it isn’t my favorite customer. I don’t know what you did but my shop was swarmed by orders after your visit. They got so popular I had to hire two full time workers to help me produce and ship them.” “Glad to be of service,” Paulette giggles, eyeing the new accessories displayed near the counter. “Is anything here to your fancy? original: You give the two aliens a cheeky smile. “I wouldn’t mind seeing another side of this moon’s raw, brutal beauty. So, what’s the plan?” Dabbebieth makes a small victory dance perched on Paulette’s spaulder as she shows you the holomap. “Irestead is here and the Shimmering Vale is there,” she says, pointing to a spot about two miles south of the city. “[flags.PAULETTE_UVETO_DATE |We’ll take the short road again. It’s more dangerous and colder but it’s the only way to get the best spots for the show.” |The safest road takes a long detour along the glacier, where the hostile locals tend to avoid going. Or, we could make it in about an hour if we cut through the big rift splitting the frozen mountain and we’ll get a better spot... but we’ll be more exposed to indigenous huntresses. I’d rather take the latter option; I don’t know how long we’ll survive the cold winds,” she admits. ] “Then we’ll trek through the canyon. We’ll just have to stay on our guard and find a nice shelter once we’re here.” The zaika nods before stowing her map in one of her layered vest’s pockets. “Alright then, let’s press on.” Without any more preparations, you step outside of Irestead’s bounds, hands close to your weapons in case you need to defend yourself against an aggressive, furry hunter. The winds howl as your [pc.isNaga |coils |[pc.legs] ] leave deep marks into the fresh snow, a sorrowful bellow that negates all hopes for any of you to take flight and glide through this pass of frosted rocks and frozen water. Your companions clearly aren’t in their element: Dabbebieth hugs the cyborg’s self-heating body the tightest he can, while the latter tries her best to stroll through the ragged terrain despite the intense chill assaulting her prosthetics. About twenty minutes after your departure from Irestead, you finally arrive where the path to the valley begins. A big stone engraved with runes indicates the way to the city, to the tundra, and to the canyon in a handful of different languages. “The dangerous part of the trip begins now. [flags.PAULETTE_UVETO_DATE |I hope the local tribes are still at peace, I wouldn’t want to risk triggering an avalanche with a stray shot |According to a stormguard ranger, the korgonne have been rather quiet for a while, implying that they could be preparing for a nasty move. I hope it won’t be the case ].” “Do you think they’ll try to ambush us?” Paulette shakes her head. “No, but the way is narrow and slippery enough to put us in trouble by itself. I’d rather not fall into a crevice because of a bad step,” she says before activating her legs’ hardlight talons. Slowly, you enter the trench dug through the mountain, eyeing any suspicious snowflake that falls too close to you. Ice covers most of the solid ore, leaving only a few layers visible through the mirroring surface. You might not be an expert in geology, but Vic showed you enough of the family business for you to identify traces of salt, silicon, and basalt in the natural fortress’s walls. Long ago, this mountain range was part of an abyssal array of volcanic chimneys, but it doesn’t tell you how it got here. Did the moon’s seas dry enough to reach the surface or did a geologic catastrophe catapult them from the oceanic floor, then through the many layers of ice, to finally pierce the surface and tower over the tundra and glaciers? Unfortunately, you don’t have the time to answer these questions. The cool winds dancing over your companions’ bodies and the growing likelihood of being attacked force you to hasten your pace. Step by step, you cross the pass, admiring the shining shards piercing through the dense coats of snow. “Those must be the phosphorescent stones the ranger told me about. The locals use them to plot safe passages for their c[silly |u |o ]mrades and to guide the souls of their ancestors through the darkest nights.” “Maybe that’s what gives the Shimmering Vale its name,” you remark as the exit from the canyon appears in front of you. After a couple more minutes of walking through the treacherous ice, you finally enter the brightly lit location. It’s a large plain stuck between ribcage-like spires of frosted, magmatic rock. Hundreds of big, stone monoliths shine with monochromatic light, turning the snow around them into a mottled palette. The foggy air hugs the pass with an iridescent mist filled with ethereal snowflakes. Dabbebieth, Paulette, and you are left gawking by this place’s raw beauty, contemplating this preserved, unaltered wonder. You spot a circle of igloos near the other entrance of the vale — taking the rougher road was the right choice. “Magnificent,” the leyak poet whispers, a flawless tune that bounces on the closest obelisk and makes it glisten with argent light. Paulette takes a few seconds to snap out of the luminescent spectacle’s hypnotic grasp. “[flags.PAULETTE_UVETO_DATE |There’s still danger in here, I think we should go to Margoth’s place if we want to spend some quality time here |Looks like there’s a small promontory dug in the left cliff. We’ll be safe there ]. Ready for a quick aerial trip?” Before you can reply, Paulette activates her hardlight wings[pc.hasWings | and takes flight. A couple of seconds later, you unfold yours and follow her through the snowy sky |, grabs you by the waist, then takes flight ]. Chill gusts caress your face as you glide together through the particle-heavy clouds, towering over domesticated beasts and shepherds as you swoop toward what must have been an auxiliary chimney dug through the peak’s side. There’s a sizable, circular home built up here, a big igloo large enough to house a family of twelve — you’ll be able to take shelter inside if it gets too cold. Paulette lands unceremoniously on the promontory[pc.hasWings |, followed by yourself a few seconds later | then carefully releases you on the snowy plateau ]. You barely have the time to appreciate your surroundings before [flags.PAULETTE_UVETO_DATE |Margoth greets you with her cheeky smile. “You again? The last time must have left a stunning impression on you,” she teases. “I’ll make us some drinks and bring you blankets before the next aurora starts. It shouldn’t be long.” |the augmented zaika aims her right arm at the frosty home’s entrance, ready to open fire. “I can see your pulse on my readings. Show yourself, I promise not to fire.” In the blink of an eye, a glowing creature surges from the ground, hardlight longbow ready to unleash a piercing arrow through the former XO’s armor. “Who are you and why did you come here?” the plump, curvy huntress shouts. She’s got a mix of essyra and leithan features: her arms and legs are clad in soft scales and seven, white-furred tails with blue-frosted tips wag behind her. She’s wearing a dense, orange and green vest that fails to hide her fat physique and chitin-covered scales. A dim, pink light comes from her plates, a light akin to the one running through her weapon. Her leithan pupils stare at you while her perky fox ears track your breathings in search for any sign of hostility. “My companions and I came here to see an aurora. We traveled through the mountain then flew here to have a better place to observe it. I’m [pc.name] and these are my friends, Paulette and Dabbebieth,” you reply, lowering your cyborg companion’s deadly arm and stepping between her and the furry archer. A moment later, she deactivates her weapon and offers you a kind smile. “Visitors usually observe it from the village down below. It’s been years since someone went through the howling canyon. The crystals down there can ruin any machinery that comes too close to them. You’re lucky it didn’t fry your limbs and systems. Oh! where are my manners? I’m Margoth, Rhenworld researcher in charge of the singing quartz’s studies. You must be frozen from the trip; I’ll fetch you some blankets and some hot chocolate before the next aurora begins,” she says before glancing at her codex. “There should be one in five minutes, so try to stay warm for a little longer.” ] The field researcher returns into her igloo as you find a nice spot to observe the vale below. As the time draws near, the glowing stones’ intensity weakens to the point of emitting no more than dim sparks that barely surface from the white coat of snow. Margoth emerges from her glacial home a couple of minutes later, her hands burdened with wooly linens, a big, fuming thermos, and metallic cups strapped to her belt. She offers Paulette and Dabbebieth the thic[silly |c |k ]est wraps before handing you a green blanket and serving the creamy beverage to her guests. “Just in time. Beware, it’s very hot.” The hybrid barely has the time to fill her own cup and to sit next to you before the show begins. You take a sip of the rich, sweet drink as the first electromagnetic wave scars the sky, followed by dozens more ethereal tides. Chromatic flames dance in front of your [pc.eyes], carried by the snowflakes and loaded particles swarming the vale’s foggy air. In a matter of seconds, the entirety of the plain turns into an iridescent supernova of colors, passing from gold, emerald, then sapphire and ruby lights at a supernatural pace. It’s a wonder this debauchery doesn’t hurt your mind. Quite the contrary instead — just looking at it soothes you and calls to your innocent inner child’s naive curiosity. “Absolutely magnificent,” the former XO gasps before taking another set of pictures with her visor and chugging more of her heart-warming beverage. The spectacle in front of her eyes is so charming, so wonderful, so resplendent that it seems to quench her body’s primal reaction to milk-based products. The furry [silly |nerd |scientist ] next to you giggles. “Wait: the best part is yet to come.” Puzzled by her remark, you focus back onto the mesmerizing waves’ sultry ballet. As soon as the electromagnetic disturbance reaches the quartz, the sensitive stones start irradiating their surroundings with crackling jolts of energy, releasing more beautiful, shimmering shards and producing a pure, immaculate melody. Dabbebieth, entranced by the exotic symphony, adds his own chant to the stormy performance, making the whole experience even more magical. The melody goes up and down, flowing through the air like tap water caressing smooth stones: a velvety caress that subjugates your [pc.ears] while the glistening rays seduce your [pc.eyes] through a crystalline mist filled with tiny, icy mirrors. As swiftly as it started, the aurora slowly fades away. The singing monoliths whisper one last time before their music stops and they start to emit their respective, monochromatic light again. The frosted hybrid is the first one to react after the show’s end, looking down the valley for any sign of trouble. “My instruments didn’t explode this time. I guess you’re my lucky charms!” she teases. “I really liked your voice, little jellyfish guy — you should consider joining an orchestra.” The blue leyak bounces on Paulette’s lap as she finishes her milky drink. “Thanks for the hot chocolate and the blankets. Is there anything we could do to repay you?” Paulette asks, a deep blush on her cheeks and an intoxicated eagerness in her voice. “Your visit already warmed my half-leithan blood — observing dangerous phenomena can be lonely from time to time, but every second is worth the trouble. I think that’s what led my mother to Uveto and to my essyra sire: sharing a wonderful time with those who mattered to her. Speaking of which, if you really want to repay me, I wouldn’t be against feeling someone’s warm touch. But beware: I’ve got some clingy tentacles and a womanhood so warm, a thermal pack will feel cold in comparison.” The choice is up to you.

Hands in hand, you follow the frilly warrior queen up to the fringes of her kingdom, sharing one last deep, sloppy kiss before returning to your quest across the stars.

original: “It may shock you, but there’s almost too much flora for my taste [var0|here|there]. Don’t get me wrong, [var0|this planet’s a|Mhen’ga would be a] zaika paradise if it weren’t for its sun’s strong kisses. A pristine haven not unlike what must have been Dhaal at some point in the past. I don’t know, maybe I’m too picky and can’t appreciate just how perfect [var0|this|the] place is, especially for a flower enthusiast like myself.” “[flags.PAULETTE_MHENGA_DATE |We could go out in the jungle again, I’m sure it’ll help you to make up your mind |How about going out in the jungle and gathering some plants then? You’ll certainly love to be in her element ].” Paulette nods. “Yeah, it’d be nice to wander through the canopies and mangroves[flags.PAULETTE_MHENGA_DATE | | all over again ]. I’ll just have to check if I’ve got enough sunscreen beforehand — my skin’s still very light-sensitive compared to a standard galactic citizen. Maybe we’ll meet some kind aliens on the way. I’d love to ruffle a zil’s fuzzy mane or play with a naleen’s [silly |noodle |serpentine lower body ], but I doubt they’ll let me have it my way. I noticed that the locals are very territorial and aggressive toward aliens — an understandable response considering how some of us treat them and how eager your companies are to ruin [var0|this|their] world just like the zaibatsu ravaged Dhaal,” she says, glowing eyes loaded with a melancholic sadness you wouldn’t expect from somebody her age. “Let’s hope [var0|this|the] planet survives their ravenous appetite for profit.”
context: <<POS:08138616>> textify(['\\n\\t<i>“It may shock you, but there’s almost too m'...])

original: [flags.TARKUS_DESTROYED |“I’d rather not talk about it. [var0|This|The] miserable pile of dust and junk was barely holding together thanks to rust and prayers sure, but it was my pile of dust and junk, in a way. I just hope they saved as many lives as possible before the Stellar Tethers broke. Nobody deserves to lose their home,” she whispers in a voice drier than a desert. |“Home sweet home,” Paulette [silly |ejaculates |snarks ] with a surprising amount of cynicism and sarcasm, “I don’t know if I’ve got anything specifically against [var0|this|the] dusty ball of broken metal and ravaged lands. Don’t get me wrong, I wouldn’t wish for anyone to be stranded [var0|here|there], and yet I didn’t feel bad or miserable there. It’s not a paradise — sure — but it’s not a hellhole either. There’s enough hostile lifeforms and machines to justify why one would resign from society and yet people live and strive together. They trust each other, eat together, work together — I even saw a gabilani and a raskvel have a makeshift wedding ceremony between two acid rains. It’s a place where beauty blooms against all odds.” She’s right: you found her and Dabbebieth [var0|here|there] after all. The blue leyak’s cheeks turn cobalt, blushing and flustered so much that it disrupts its flight. “You filthy tease, I’ll have you know I was transplanted [var0|here|to Tarkus], not homegrown. It reminds me of the Gyre in some ways: a horrible, dangerous place in which the worst or the best can happen. Be it a zaika ganger or a vicious sydian, chances are you’ll get robbed of some chrome at a moment’s notice — if a rough machine doesn’t crush you first that is. Tarkus isn’t a place for softies, but it reveals if we are monsters or heroes.” So... which one is she? “I don’t know, but I certainly met my heroes,” she says, glancing at Dabbebieth, then longingly gazing at you. ]
context: <<POS:08140440>> textify(['\\n\\t[flags.TARKUS_DESTROYED\\n\\t\\t\\t|<i>“I’d rather'...])

original: [var0 |“I’d rather not talk about it. I hoped that the Myr and the U.G.C weren’t cold-blooded enough to allow a massacre, but here we are. It’s such a shame that their story in the great theater of life ended like this, their strings cut just before reaching a potentially nicer act. Stars above, I didn’t have any real friends [var1|down ]there but I still feel like something was taken away from me,” she confesses, “they deserved a chance to see the stars, to live a fulfilling life, to make the galaxy a better place.” |“On the one hand, I really appreciate the humid tunnels’ darkness, on the other, it breaks my heart to know that [var1|this world’s been|Myrellion has been] — and still is — so close to turning into a glassed graveyard. War ruins everything: plants, fields, cities, culture, art, and even people. Especially the people. I’d have loved to see the raw, unpolished wonder [var1|this|the] planet was before the Gold and Red Myr let their spite ravage their homes.” She’s very emotional about it. Did something similar happen to her homeworld? Paulette shrugs. “I’m not sure, only the Peers have access to a good enough education to learn our species’ - and our world’s — history. One lone, cold night on Tarkus, I browsed through the UGC’s reports, looking for data related to Dhaal. According to their orbital scans and publicly-released documents, it’s been tidally-locked for eons and life didn’t bloom on it before — it’s almost terrifying to know that our world had to become a volcanic mess and a glacial hellhole for its equator to flourish with biological marvels. I’d love to see the terraforming projects come to life and grant us tropical forests all over our home. I hope the Myrs, Bothriocs, and Nyrea will find a way to unite and build a civilisation that’ll turn their scarred world into a shining beacon of peace.” ]
context: <<POS:08142644>> textify(['\\n\\t[var0\\n\\t\\t\\t|<i>“I’d rather not talk about it'...])

original: “A lair of scoundrels, ne’er-do-well, and slaves. I don’t know if I should love or hate [var0|this|that] place.” Weird answer considering she used to be a slave to a very brutal owner. Can she elaborate? Paulette runs an orange hand through her projected plumage. “It reminds me of Dhaal in some ways: the rampant criminality, the lawlessness, the fear of being enslaved or beaten down at any corner — all [var0|these|those] struggling lives, broken dreams, and injustices feel way too real for my little zaika heart. Alone, I wouldn’t be able to save more than a handful of souls and even so, I’m not sure we could fly away from all the cutthroats. But I’d gladly make a ruckus just to see some pirates abducted by their former playthings and turned into drooling cumdumps,” she says, her eyes glowing with a devilish light. [var0|You’ll have to be even more careful up here, chances are there’ll be a lot more random brawls and surprise uprisings with her in the vicinity.]
context: <<POS:08144804>> textify(['\\n\\t<i>“A lair of scoundrels, ne’er-do-well, and s'...])

original: “It’s weird [var0|to be back here|every time I go back there], nothing[var0|| has] changed and yet everything [var0|did|has].” Could she elaborate? Paulette looks down on her orange limbs, a deep melancholy burning in her eyes. “Even though I’m free from my burdened past, I still feel powerless and minuscule... crushed by the Gyre’s giant buildings and choking on acrid smoke as if I were a nameless factory worker all over again. Don’t get me wrong, I’m very glad to live with you[crewRange 2 | and your friends, ] but deep down I feel like I’m still the weak, frail zaika I used to be.[flags.PAULETTE_DHAAL_DATE | Seeing Lappis and Harvvey again helped a little but I can’t stop myself from thinking that I — out of all of mom’s daughters — was the one who failed her. ]” Her blue leyak friend slowly presses his face against her right cheek and wraps his tentacles around her shoulders in what must pass for a tight hug for him while you place a comforting hand over her clawed chrome. “You’ve gone through a lot. It’s a miracle you didn’t give up and let yourself rot on Tarkus. I’m sure your adoptive mother would be proud of the zaika you’ve become.” “Thank you [pc.name], for everything — and to you as well, my floating friendo,” she whispers, dragging you all together in a snug hug. “I wouldn’t be here without you... I don’t think I’ll ever be able to repay you.” “As far as I’m concerned, we’re even. I don’t know about Dabbebieth’s point of view though, he might be a little bit more demanding for babysitting you for so long.” “I’m sure we’ll come to a mutual understanding,” she teases, her doubts and sadness but a distant souvenir already.
context: <<POS:08146000>> textify(['\\n\\t<i>“It’s weird [var0|to be back here|every tim'...])

original: Paulette shivers. “[var0|This|That] place, it scares me down to my bones, metallic ones included.” It[var0|’s scaring| scares] her? How so? “It’s not about the radiation-heavy wastes or the raiders roaming them that freezes me to my core... it’s what happened to [var0|this|that] place. What — or rather, who’s responsible for [var0|this|that] planet’s state terrifies me. To think that one day, something can absolutely ruin the shit out of an entire planet and doom its surviving denizens to a life of strife and suffering, that fills me with pure dread.” Yeah, voiced like this, it’s indeed scary. Doesn’t she feel awe at the local flora and fauna’s resilience? The former XO tilts her head. “I do, but it only deepens my worries. They are so strong, so inventive, so alive, fighting tooth and nail to claim [var0|these|the] glassed deserts and poisoned oases — if they can be so powerful and resilient, the one that disfigured [var0|this|the] planet must be capable of anything. Promise me you’ll be careful out there.” “[pc.kmhbb |Don’t worry Paulette, I won’t do anything stupid[var0| out there],” you reply, placing a comforting hand on her cybernetic thigh |I’ll try to avoid troubles, but [silly |the scenario wants me to be an issue magnet |I seem to attract dangerous weirdos,” you tease ]” |Getting into trouble runs in the family. You don’t have to worry, I’m the one who knocks[silly | | up ],” you boast |Don’t worry Polly cutie, I’ll stay out of trouble. It’ll be vewwy hard if trouble’s got some meaty bitsies though,” you giggle |I won’t flex over my foes too much, I promise,” you grunt ]. Your zaika crewmate smiles, not completely reassured but charmed by your natural charisma and confidence. “Y-You know what? Fine, I trust your fighting skills and survival instincts. You wouldn’t have come this far if you were a frail, weak, and pathetic [pc.race] after all,” she says — a weird way to compliment your abilities. “Maybe we could go out together — you know — to better protect ourselves while we discover [var0|this planet’s|Phaedra’s] peculiar growths and landscapes,” she says, with darkening cheeks. It’s unsettling how such a strong, deadly cyborg can behave like a blushing violet whenever she tries to do something slightly romantic.
context: <<POS:08147948>> textify(['\\n\\tPaulette shivers. <i>“[var0|This|That] place, '...])

original: “What do you think of New Texas, the pastoral paradise?” you say, making the former XO startle. “I-I’ve conflicting feelings about the place. On the one hand, I’d love to live in a place so verdant and blooming with life but on the other hand, [var0|this place’s|the place is] just a giant trap for weak-willed zaikas.” “Because of the milk?” “Indeed. Even a heavily chromed-up Paragon Peer or Bright Water [silly |nerd |scientist ] would lose it in the span of a day. Send a colonial ship [var0|here|there] and you’ll have a zaika petslave per household by the end of the day, I could bet my legs on it.” Did she visit the Governor’s Ranch and if so, how was it? “I did. It would have been better if doing so didn’t turn me into the biggest piece of entertainment, though. The barman told me I would make for a perfect new recruit for the Living Kegs’ ranks — an offer I politely declined even though I’d look stunning in cowprint lingerie,” she teases, an amethyst blush burning her cheeks. “I also brought this fancy hat back[var0| from the surface], but I don’t know where I snatched it,” she adds, pointing at a cowboy hat branded with the security forces’ golden star — chances are she’s now a New Texas Ranger.
context: <<POS:08150598>> textify(['\\n\\t<i>“What do you think of New Texas, the pastor'...])

original: “I[var0||’ve] talked with some of Canadia Station’s workers; the place sounds lovely.” Interesting. Is she thrilled by the cool climate and the resilient flora or rather by the large amount of tauric individuals populating it? The zaika’s cheeks take a deep, amethyst blush. “I-I don’t see what you mean. I’m sincerely fond of what the settlers have done with [var0|this|the] place. The fact that their society was designed around the cohabitation of tauric and bipedal citizens makes it a very endearing proof of intelligence and wisdom. I wish we had the same luck back on Dhaal. We could be developing in a verdant, bountiful paradise rather than spreading a dark, industrialized hellscape spread across our world’s equator. My younger self would have killed to smell the peculiar scent of a pine or a maple tree. I’ll let you imagine what I would have done to live surrounded by them.” Yeah, you can see what she means. Some people will do anything to escape their shackles. Maybe you’ll spend some well-earned holidays on Vesperia with her once your quest will be over. “Count me in. But be warned, I might get accustomed to flannel shirts and cozy fireplaces,” she teases.
context: <<POS:08152095>> textify(['\\n\\t<i>“I[var0||’ve] talked with some of Canadia S'...])

original: “Stars, [var0|this moon’s|Uveto is] colder than Dhaal’s dark hemisphere. The fact that locals can survive the freezing gusts without wearing heat belts just drives me insane!” So you take it she’d rather stay [var0|up here|far away], where it’s warm and comfy? Paulette nods. “Yeah but a mean little guy keeps pushing me to go planetside[var0|| whenever we’re nearby],” she grumbles, sending Dabbebieth a death stare. The floating leyak retorts by offering her a mocking face that makes the purple XO even madder. “He’s even more vulnerable to the frost than I am and yet he keeps begging me to lead him through the glaciers in search of inspiration. Don’t get me wrong, I’d love to see [flags.PAULETTE_UVETO_DATE |another magical |an ] aurora under a snowy sky but [silly |the cold really bothers me, FUCK YOU ELSA!!!! |I’m not geared for arctic exploration ].” “So you’re telling me the dreadful cyborg’s scared of going outside in the snow? I thought you were tougher than that. I guess I’m friends with a chilly coward,” you tease. The chromed-up cutie pouts. “I-I’ll show you who’s a chilly coward. [var0|Next time we’re there, ]I dare you to meet us at Irestead’s exit for a polar trek, then we’ll see who’s gonna beg the other to go back to the ship,” she roars, a bravado that was intended to be scary but rendered funny in its delivery. “As long as Dabbebieth wears a fluffy cap, I’ll be happy to travel Uveto’s permafrost with you.”
context: <<POS:08153507>> textify(['\\n\\t<i>“Stars, [var0|this moon’s|Uveto is] colder '...])

original: Which planet will you ask her about?
context: <<POS:08155197>> output("Which planet will you ask her about?")

original: “Maybe it’s me but the ship feels a lot more inviting since we gathered these mhen’gan plants. It’s a bit weird because they take a lot of space and yet the [pc_ship.short] feels a lot bigger and welcoming now.” Paulette nods. “Indeed, it’s a wonder I can’t really explain either — I think that’s what it’s for people who grew up in a polluted megalopolis devoid of non-zaika life. I’m really proud of how our chosen growths made my room — and by extension your home — so agreeable to live in. I even think that the life support systems work better with them aboard.” You’ll have to check on that later, just to be sure your crew isn’t in danger. By the way, can she tell you more about her vegetal babies? “Sure, they are hungry little ones but absolutely lovely once they are growing and blooming with life. With some luck, the Mhen’gan mango tree will bear its first fruits in six or seven years but for now, its large leaves and sturdy trunk provide us with a lot of fresh air. It hurts me to admit it, but I love it more than my small Saole tree, and stars do I like its bark’s smell and the humid clouds it produces when it’s well ‘fed’. I don’t know why, but I feel at home when I’m close to this tropical wonder. Maybe it reminds my body of what my ancestors’ life was as agile tree-dwellers of our planet’s exotic equator.” You slowly nod. It’s not unheard of that ausar or kaithrit feel melancholic near flora that reminds them of their homeworld, even if they never step a [silly |pawb |foot ] on it. It wouldn’t surprise you if zaika had the same kind of reaction. Speaking of reactions, the flowers and liana she picked grew a lot since she placed them all over the corridors. Does she have an explanation for why they’re invading your flying home? The purple cyborg tilts her head. “That’s because I cut them often and transplant them in other rooms when they grow big to the point of collapsing. The Porui ivy becomes invasive if it’s too dark and Fah’ku anemones die as soon as the air around them is too dry, so shaping the former so that it’s always exposed to some form of dim light and keeping the latter far enough from them ensures that they won’t thrust their roots into sensitive and vulnerable subsystems. The ivy blooms with beautiful flowers that have a very distinctive scent when they’re exposed to a strong lighting and the anemones wiggle and diffuse a soothing scent when it’s dark — a bit like natural pomanders with extra steps. I really like to see them when I get to sleep or wake up. I feel like I’m allowed to be here rather than a creature manufactured to serve someone else’s purpose,” she confesses. “[pc.kmhbb |You’re your own person, with your own goals and dreams. Never forget that |If I’m correct, you replaced your standard XO bits with these slender, bone-crushing chrome augs. You’re a lot less powerless than you think |Oh really? The threatening cyborg that had no reason to be kind made friends and followed them through the stars is the powerless one? You’ve got some weird conclusions |I don’t feel like you’re a stringless dollie; you’re a tough cookie full of love and prettiness |You survived with a cybernetic body in the middle of the sydians’ territory. You’re not as powerless as you think ],” you reply, bringing a slight smile to her face. “I-I guess you’re right. Maybe living somewhere that isn’t a glorified junk pile made me realize I had worth again, that I deserve to be happy and to have nice things too. Thank you for that [pc.name].” The pleasure’s all yours. She’s welcome to stay as long as she wants, as long as she takes care of her luscious babies and brightens the crew’s life of course. “Count on me- I mean... on us. Dabbebieth’s been less shy around my Saole tree, maybe he’ll sing us something sweet now that he’s got beautiful, delicate muses to caress his artistic fiber.”
context: <<POS:08155320>> textify(['\\n\\t<i>“Maybe it’s me but the ship feels a lot mor'...])

original: Looking around Paulette’s quarters, you notice an ever growing pile of cheap novels defying gravity in one of the room’s corners. The colorful covers fill almost all of her available space outside of her pillow fort of a bed[flags.PAULETTE_MHENGA_DATE | and plants ]. Doesn’t she want a holo-reader instead of these cumbersome hard covers? “It’s nice of you to propose it to me, but no. I prefer to read stuff on real support rather than looking at a glowing screen.” Does she prefer to feel the singular touch of paper on her robotic hands or is there something else behind it? The former XO nods. “There’s some of that in the balance. Feeling the weight of the words in your hands as you slowly decipher the characters or narrator’s emotions and intentions has a weird appeal I can’t really put a precise word on. It also forces us to take care of our belongings and to respect the sheets that bear and embody our fictional universes — it’s a relation you can’t get with a lightweight, technological device in which thousands if not millions of stories can hold together inside it. Don’t get me wrong, I wouldn’t be against acquiring one for myself, but I’d rather have tangible evidence that I’m interested in a certain topic or story. Furthermore, the displays are usually far too bright for my zaika eyes. We used to be tree-dwellers living in the twilight of Dhaal’s equator. It’s already hard for us to deal with the Gyre’s neon lights so I’ll let you imagine how tough it’s to sustain long exposure to sunlight, even for an augmented peacekeeper.” “Yeah, I can understand that. Do you have any favorite stories or topics?” The zaika’s cheeks take an amethyst color. “N-Nothing particularly interesting,” she babbles, a weird answer that forces you to take a closer look at a few of the hard covers lying on her bed table. You notice a book about forestry, one on robotics, and five romance books, most of them featuring cute boys or nerdy girls becoming [silly |based |famous ] kinksters. “I-I need lots of data to take care of my chromed-up body[flags.PAULETTE_MHENGA_DATE | and sensitive plants’ |’s ] needs. It’s hard for me to understand how the rest of the galaxy works,” she babbles, trying her best to hide how flustered she’s but failing so hard her leyak roommate looks open and composed in comparison. It’s rather touching to see such a big, powerful lady turn into a blushing violet if you rub her the correct way. “Fine then, keep your secrets,” you tease, easing the tension on the poor cyborg but not enough to let her go off the hook. “I hope you’ll make good use of your new knowledge.” The former peacekeeper nods. “Count on me [pc.name], it’s the least I can do to repay you for everything you did for us,” she mutters, reaching for her shy friend’s feelers and kindly dragging him close to her chest. “I know someone who might love to have a brand new holopad to write and listen to poetry on,” she adds, caressing Dabbebieth’s gelatinous forehead while the sentient alien slowly nods with his entire body. You’ll think about it the next time you’ll be safe in the galactic core, far from the reach of any dubious entities trying to get their hands on a[pc.kmhbb |n innocent |n ingenuous | tough | charming | strapping ] [pc.race].
context: <<POS:08159495>> textify(['\\n\\tLooking around Paulette’s quarters, you notice'...])

original: Can she tell you more about Lithium Flower? Paulette frowns. “I’d rather not, but if you really want, I can make an exception. What do you need to know?” Who she is, to begin with. “One of Paragon’s top scientists. Back when I was still her pet-bodyguard, she was head of the cybernetic research department. If it weren’t for her genetic impairment, she’d certainly be the zaibatsu’s maven.” So she didn’t mod herself to be smaller? Paulette shakes her head. “She was, and certainly still is, very anal about it. Whenever someone brought height or size up, she would shout I’M NOT SMALL!” Talk about being in denial. “How did you end up in her custody?” [flags.EGG_END |Your crewmate frowns. “[pc.name], I think you need your head checked. You were here with us aboard the E.G.G and you read the same classified documents. She’s the one that inducted me into the GU project and personally modified me to be the [silly |Ultimate Lifeform |next step to zaika evolution ]. |Your crewmate shrugs. “I... don’t remember. I think that she was part of the team that altered me? I recall her rich apartments and properties. She was so precious with her belongings... a real brat, always eager to get new toys to play with. When the fashion was natural, short hair and giver-inclined, she always wore the most risque tops and shorts. Her poor clothes were almost on the verge of tearing. Out of all the things she had, I think I was the only one that she didn’t consider disposable for some reason. ]” “I only remember how cramped and sad my tank was,” Dabbebieth adds. “She knew that I was a sapient being, but she kept me underfed on purpose. If it weren’t for my sudden kidnapping, I’d still be a mindless animal.” Paulette gently caresses her roommate’s sails. “There there, it’s over now. The small, mean lady won’t hurt you anymore.” [silly |Weird. You could have sworn someone shouted I’M NOT SMALL! ] Dabbebieth pouts. “Don’t treat me like a child. I’m older than you are!” “To be honest, you’re more like a cat: sleeping all day, waking only to eat and yowling melancholically from atop your pillow,” she teases. The little leyak breathes deeply, filling himself like a puffer fish. “How rude! You deserve a stern correction, you little brat,” he angrily babbles, waving his tentacles around. He tries hard to look strong and menacing but you can’t find him anything but cute. Maybe that’s how Lithium Flower felt around other zaika? Paulette tenses again. “Lithium Flower’s always been a spiteful goblin. When she couldn’t overpower someone with her tendrils, she’d call me to neutralize them for her. She made me do horrible things, and she did despicable things to me, in turn. I’ll never forgive her, even though we settled it in ritual combat – what she did to me left an indelible scar on my body and soul.” “And you left a giant dent in her ego,” Dabbebieth softly replies. “Few have done this and lived to brag about it.” “True. But if I’m honest, we beat her as a team. Without you, I don’t know where I’d be right now, or if I’d still be a-” You place a finger on her pillowy lips. “I’m sure that you’d be fine. After all, you had the best teammate to support you,” you remark, making Dabbebieth blush deeply. Paulette smiles. “Yeah, you’re probably right. original: When you enter Paulette’s quarters, you are greeted by a cold, solemn zaika. Your usually cheerful and smiling companion stands still, not a single muscle or artificial fiber moving an inch — you can barely see her lungs inflate. Dabbebieth gently waves at you from her shoulder, a reassuring sight that makes his roommate look even more concerned and worried. “[pc.name], a word please.” Her voice feels as emotionless as her body, almost as if her XO subroutines took over and wiped her personality away. Slowly, you take her hand and guide her to her [silly |pillow fort of a |plush ] bed. The violet cyborg shivers in your grasp; she’s scared — no, terrified — by something so much that it’s left her duller than a tern winter sun. The powerful warrior is gone, replaced by a shaking little girl surrounded by nightmares. “What’s the matter, Paulette? [pc.kmhbb |It’s the first time I see you like this. |Did somebody pick your nose? |I expected you to be tougher than that. |Who hurt my smart cookie? |Who took your smile away? ]” Her electric blue eyes stare at you for long, silent seconds before setting on a small hologram projected by her left arm. The orange-tinted image of a tidally-locked planet flanked by a set of interstellar coordinates you immediately recognize. “Dhaal.” “Yes. Dhaal, my homeworld. The moment I saw them, everything came back: the twilight sky, the factories belching acrid smog, the transformation... the pain — her wicked smile.” Paulette’s lips continue to move but no sound comes from them, only the visceral tremors in her core survive her body’s defensive censorship. [pc.isAss |Reluctantly |Wordlessly ], you hug the poor zaika tight. Her heart races and her chest barely rises. It’s a miracle she didn’t snap despite how strong her panic attack is. Your warmth and closeness paired with Dabbebieth’s tentacle brushes are an isle of calm and serenity she desperately holds to as the tsunami of emotions relentlessly try to claim her back. “It’s okay Paulette, you aren’t alone anymore. Whatever shadow or demon of your past tries to hurt you, we’ll take them down, together.” “B-But what if I’m not strong enough? My body might be performing normally, but it doesn’t have the power to dent an XO, to hurt something harder — and that’s not taking my reduced speed and shielding into account. I’ll be a dead weight if we face my people’s war machines,” she whispers. Slowly, you shake your head. “You’ll never be a dead weight to me,” you reassure her, running your hand across her back and through her tied locks. “If you need more power, I can get you some new gear. Stuff they won’t expect to face and that’ll give you the upper hand.” The alien cyborg set her misty eyes on you, trying hard to keep it up. “[pc.name]... thank you. I’m so sorry for being so weak and useless right now,” she mutters, straightening her back and taking deep breaths. You drop a small kiss on her cheek then break the embrace, keeping her left hand in yours as you rise from the blanket-covered mattress. “What’ll you need to update your systems?” “I-If you really want to do this, I’ll need an energy weapon that shoots thermal, electric, and cryo energy and an agility-enhancing shield, of pirate origin if possible — to be sure they won’t expect it.” Is there anything else she needs? [var0 |Paulette tilts her head. “There’s something Dabbebieth and I need to do before we go back to my homeworld. We’ve talked about it before meeting you and it’s really important to us. If we are to return to Dhaal, we want to do it as free U.G.C. citizens, not as runaway slaves. There’s an administrator on Mhen’ga that can put us through the expedited uplift-citizenship tests. I’d like to go there before thinking of going back to Dhaal |“Not really.. ]. Be careful out there [pc.name]. I don’t want to lose you.” “Don’t do anything I wouldn’t do, then,” you tease, blowing a kiss at her. The worried zaika smiles as she catches it, a dim smile that bears just enough hope and faith in you to let you leave. Before she can spiral again, Dabbebieth reaches her with his tentacles, distracting her with his tickling touch while you consider your next move. Paulette’s former owner might come for them if you land on Dhaal, and she’ll surely be with a tough retinue. You better prepare for what’s to come — even if she doesn’t stand a chance against you.

original: “I’ve got what you need,” you say, handing the tri-beam rifle and Jumper shield to Paulette. The zaika’s face darkens as she scours the pirate gear in your hands. She knows that there won’t be any ways to turn back if she takes them. “Thank you [pc.name]. I’m sorry for troubling you with my problems,” she mutters before recovering the shield belt and energy weapon. “Can you please fetch my cannon arm? I left it in my bathroom.” Slowly, you rise from the mattress and head toward the adjacent room while Paulette fumbles with the cumbersome rifle. You easily find her combat arm in her orderly bathroom. It’s been cleansed and polished recently, you would almost be amazed by how shiny the Annihilator is if it weren’t such a potentially devastating weapon. Carefully, you take the Pyrexxx-brand armament and step back to the cyborg’s bunk. In the short time you were away, she unfolded her hardlight wings’ armature and deconstructed the tri-beam rifle and Jumper shield. The former’s inners are sprayed across the blankets and the latter’s buckle has been replaced with an intricate set of hardlight loops. “Oh, you found it already?” she gasps, recovering her cannon and offering you the modified belt to you. “Can you please attach the shield’s centerpiece to my wings’ base and clip the rest to the frame while I upgrade my arm?” You slowly nod then slither behind her back. You’d expect it to be a bit complicated to fit the altered device onto her wings, but the generator and loops slide almost perfectly into the gaps in her metallic limbs, as if they were designed to welcome new modules. Maybe the original Pyrexxx designs were intended to be equipped with more advanced features or designed to be customized — that wouldn’t surprise you, from the combat sexbot manufacturers. A surprised shiver shakes Paulette the moment the pirate shield interfaces with her body. “Stars, I feel the [silly |warp |power ] overtaking me,” she groans, fitting the juiced-up cannon on her arm socket. A second later, the increased gun’s length glows with prismatic light, as if she were about to unleash a deadly salvo. “I’m barely fueling it — it’s [silly |like having the power of the sun in the palm of my hand |so strong I could tear through the Nova’s stellar shields ]. I could get used to this,” she mumbles before reluctantly swapping her combat arm for her usual prosthetic. “Sorry for that [pc.name], I got a little carried away. Power and might do things to people, especially if they used to be the dispossessed, helpless ones before. I think I — no, we — will make it if my past comes back to bite my ass. Is there something I can do to repay you for the trouble?” You might have some ideas...

original: As you walk through your ship’s guts, you notice Paulette and Anno sharing a warm drink in the meeting room. “So what happens if you remove one of your limbs? Do you feel its absence?” Paulette shrugs. “Not really. My original arms and legs were replaced so long ago that I don’t have any phantom pain anymore. I don’t even know if I had any to begin with, since I wasn’t in position to have any control over my body or emotions back then. My limbs are just here or not.” “Fascinating. Does your behavior change with what’s attached to your body?” The former XO tilts her head. “What do you mean?” Anno lifts her fuzzy arm. “Let’s imagine that you’re offered warm, soft, fur-covered prosthetics instead of your rough, sharp, military grade ones — would you act differently?” “I-I guess,” the zaika replies, a dark blush setting on her cheeks. “I might have felt a bit cuter and frailer the last time I used my [flags.PAULETTE_KITTY |kitty gear |‘special gear’ ], far from how I usually feel with my orange plate. It’s really weird, because these attachments don’t interact on a hormonal level and yet they make me feel like I’m a totally different person,” she admits. Before the purple cyborg can elaborate any further, your [anno.isHuskar |huskar |ausar ] crewmate notices you. “Hey Boss, come and take a drink with us! We were about to address weird physiology questions regarding prosthetics used in robotic petpl-” “The Dr. Dorna means how o-one’s behavior c-changes when their body does, especially when the t-transformation isn’t microsurgeon-based.” Can she tell you what her stance on it is then? Paulette coughs awkwardly. “Well... I do feel different, but I suspect any TF junkie would think the same.” “What’s interesting is that our zaika roommate has so little resistance to changing her cybernetic appearance. Most of the cyborgs or advanced A.I.s I met or studied for Steele Tech had a surprising amount of self-awareness and affection toward their attached limbs and are very reluctant to remove or replace them — even for short periods of time. I’m not an expert in psychology, but I think she’s got a very empathetic relation to her body, especially since she was experimented on and robbed of it. I’d love to verify my claims with a small experience — nothing fancy, just monitoring your vitals and brain activity with your ‘regular body’ and with your [flags.PAULETTE_KITTY |kitty gear |‘special gear’ ] on.” Paulette’s chest swells at the prospect of doing something intimate with another person, a very charming one at that. “If you’re going to do this, don’t hesitate to ask for my input,” you tease, flustering the blushing zaika even more. “Sure thing Boss, maybe I’ll monitor myself as well for the second part of the study,” she adds before finishing her cup. It’s nice to see your crewmates get along, even if it increases the risks of turning your ship into a sticky mess filled with moaning — or rather, barking and mewling pets.

original: Weird noises lure you to the engine room, clinking metal and gears grinding in a very concerning, asynchronous rhythm. Fortunately, it’s not your ship’s reactor or systems that are involved, but your zaika and raskvel crewmates busy on the former’s orange arm. “Damn girl, look at how dirty these false muscles are! No wonder your moves look so rough and basic,” the short mechanic remarks while she applies a nice sheen of oil all over the pistons lining Paulette’s detached limb. “I’ve seen a lot of cyborgs and tech fiends on Novahome but you’re the first one to use actual companion droid parts. Wanna talk about it?” Paulette shrugs. “It’s nothing fancy, really. These Pyrexxx products are cheap, powerful, energy efficient, and easy to maintain — things you’ll be looking for if you live in alone with nobody else to rely on. Dabbebieth might be a nerdy little poet and a genius when it comes to empathic behavior but he knows nothing about robotics and logistics. I had to rely on my basic knowledge to keep an operational body. And as you know, basic knowledge means little to no access to advanced systems or optimized machinery.” Before Shekka can retort, you join them and hand a screwdriver to the hyperactive raskvel. “Hey [pc.name], how long have you been here? I’m almost done updating the Ghod’Mluss’ipn’s synthetic bits and teaching her how to repair them. It’s a miracle she could move at all considering the low-quality of her prosthetics and the replacement parts she used to fix them.” You explain that you just arrived, how long have they been at it? “Maybe a couple of hours? I didn’t keep track of time,” Paulette confesses. “I was too busy appreciating how my legs move now that my gyrators are perfectly balanced[silly |, as all things shall be | again ]. I think Shekka spent half of the time on my hardlight wings.” The pink raskvel nods. “The projectors’ synchronicity was completely out of place. I wonder how you could even use them as anything other than cumbersome shields. I couldn’t do miracles with the plates’ alloys but I gave her the best synth-muscles and robotic organs on the market — black markets included. It’s fortuitous that her dhaalian design was compatible with the U.G.C’s standardized ports... it’s as if her race and culture developed following blueprints exactly the same way as the rest of the galaxy did — but I’ve got a lot of doubts about the latter’s likelihood.” “M-Mother told me that the zaibatsu’s rule over Dhaal was fairly recent in the planet’s history and the publicly available data on my homeworld mention a quick development of our society a few years after scouting probes reached the sector so... that might be it. In the end, it doesn’t really matter, unless your tech comes with a surprise backdoor that can make my homeworld a better place,” she says as Shekka finishes polishing her left arm and fits it back to her joint. “And voila! It’s as good as new — if not better. Thanks for thinking about me for fixing your body. I had a pretty good time experimenting with your prosthetics’ systems and I learned a lot from it. With some funds and some time, I could craft a big, powerful exoskeleton for myself, too — for research purposes of course,” the tiny mechanic mutters. Your zaika crewmate tests her fingers and arm’s new swiftness before turning a blushing face to her smaller friend. “Thanks a lot. It feels so light and flexible now, it’s almost surreal. By the way, could you please let go of my tail?” Shekka’s cheeks turn crimson as she realizes what her augmented tail’s been up to, quickly withdrawing it from her purple c[silly |u |o ]mrade’s sensitive limb before standing up together and stretching their sore bodies. “S-Sorry for the inconvenience, I get a little carried away when I’m dealing with fascinating technologies,” she confesses. “Let’s go to the board room; we can test your chrome on the way and I could really use a drink after all this work. The offer stands for you too, [pc.name].” “Maybe another time. I wouldn’t want to disturb you two lovebirds,” you tease, making Paulette’s chest swell and Shekka’s [shekka.hasCock |horsecock |tail ] twitch. The two of them storm together out of the room, leaving you behind amidst the humming engines. It’s endearing to see how nerdy and flustered the rask can become as soon as gadgets are involved, especially if the gadget in question is a heavily-chromed up roommate respecting her skills.

original: A potent, smoky scent lures you to the ship’s kitchen. The tasty smell coming from the boiling pot isn’t very unorthodox since you got your own chef[yammi.isCrew |s ] on board but there’s something special today, something more exotic, more out of place, more refined. Determined to find out what’s brewing and to maybe have a pleasant dish, you quickly [pc.isNaga |slither |stride ] through your moving home’s bulkheads up until your [pc.eyes] set on a very busy Dizzy seasoning a thic[silly |c |k ] plate with the help of a hyper focused Paulette wearing a frilly, bright pink apron. “And for the finishing touch, a tablespoon of creamy, Liashu juice-based sauce,” the former XO mumbles while her orange hand glazes the pan fried vegetables and carbs with the ruby concoction. She’s rather skilled, as if she were a cook in a past life, a cook almost matching your kaithrit matriarch[yammi.isCrew | and sparadat chef ]’s skills.Your chromed-up crewmates notice your presence just as they were about to sit down and dig into their dishes. “Hey there [pc.name]. Would you like to eat with us? I can serve you a nice, big plate of Paulette’s gorgeous cuisine. She’s got all it takes to be a great cook,” the fat feline remarks, bringing a slight blush to her c[silly |u |o ]mrade’s cheek. “I-It’s nothing fancy at all, really. I used to be the one in charge of the food with Mother when I was younger — we didn’t have much, but she always found ways to turn basic rations into a Peer-class meal.” Intrigued by the weird mix of colorful nuts, sliced vegetables, and grilled potatoes, you take a seat and let Dizzy serve you a rather generous portion fresh from the steamy pots. Sublime scents fill your nostrils moments before the three of you dig into the juicy delicacies, flaring your taste buds with a pleasant mix of sweet and salty flavors exhausted by the rich, ruby cream topping it. It must be a recipe from Paulette’s homeworld since you haven’t heard of Liashu fruits in the core or fringe worlds at large — a real shame considering how its unique flavor turns this simple dish into a savory explosion. “I’m glad the end result feels so balanced,” the zaika confesses, “I tried to recreate my favorite meal but we couldn’t find most of the original ingredients. It makes for a good proxy but I feel like something’s missing.” Dizzy tilts her head. “I think it’s fine as is. Adding or removing anything would ruin it in my opinion. By the way, can you tell me what your little blue friend likes? I’d like to make something special for him.” “Dabbebieth? Truth be told, I don’t know what he likes in terms of food. He siphons my tail or breasts to keep his cognitive abilities up but I really have no clue of what leyaks appreciate beside thic[silly |c |k ], protein-heavy fluids or jellies.” The mature kaithrit thinks for a couple of seconds before resuming her meal, certainly pondering what to do with this data. It doesn’t take you long to finish your plates and to wash them clean of the remaining traces of its exotic dressing. You can see how happy your two crewmates are together, behaving like mother and daughter even though their races are nothing alike. It’s quite endearing to see them have such a good alchemy, how complementary they are to one another as they prepare the ingredients for what you suspect will be a mango milkshake. “Thanks for the meal you two. I’ll see you later,” you say, dropping a quick peck on the busy ladies’ cheeks and hugging them tight before leaving them to their devices. The moment you leave the room, you hear an ecstatic whine produced by the former XO, a moan not unlike the one of a cowgirl being milked — this special zaika treat’s getting saucier by the second.

Anyway, where can I apply for the tests?” Before you can answer, a chromed Xhelarfog greets you. “Welcome to the Steele Tech Research lab. They have a high potential, so it’s only fair you’d be the one to test them first,” he says, reaching for a portable scanner. “Please, stay still for about ten seconds. The chimeric researcher connects it to his Codex and consults a couple holo-screens filled with intricate lines of code. “Did you feel an unusual warmth while I examined you? Her heavy eyelids pin her down as much if not more than her huge, swaying tits.[silly|[flags.PAULETTE_MYR_DATE| Mr. Cuddlydoo, her orange plushie, smiles at you from between her violet cleavage — lucky bastard!]] She isn’t wearing pants either, and she’s clearly got a left leg in place of her right one. Somebody had a nightmare — and a pretty bad one at that! “[pc.name]?” she yawns, doing her best to stay awake. Her chin hovers dangerously close to her bowl of honeyed porridge — it’s a miracle she didn’t have an oat-filled [silly |facial |bath ] yet. “Y-You look funny. Did you see a ghost?” “[pc.kmhbb |Paulette, you’re the one looking like a revenant,” you reply. |Pleased to meet you, Ms. Spirit. [silly |Booooobies? |A dullahan ]?” |Can I play with your Booobies Ms. Ghosty?” you reply. |Can I play with your Booobies Ms. Ghosty?” you reply. ] The cyborg blinks, confused. She frowns, lifting her spoon at eye level. She gawks in shock at her own reflection. “Stars, I look like a piece of trash! This day can’t get worse.” What’s the matter? Did something happen? “I... memories came back during my nap — unpleasant ones.” [pc.isAss |You shrug. She’s a strong girl, she can handle some post traumatic stress. Your Codex blips, picking up a potential threat. The screen chirps with warning signs for biohazards, chemhazards, and crushing hazards, all pointing at your purple crewmate. |You step closer and give the shocked zaika a hug. Her heart races in her augmented chest, [silly |pumping like a roaring Straight-69 engine |beating as if she were fighting for her life ]. ] You can only imagine the harrowing horrors she saw to be in this state. “Wanna talk about it?” “I... don’t know,” Paulette whispers. “Everything’s so confused and chaotic in my head.” Dabbebieth smoothly floats through the room and lands on Paulette’s left shoulder. “If you keep these bad memories buried, they’ll slowly gnaw their way into you, devouring everything you love, rotting you from the inside until you are nothing but a chromed husk. No need to fret, you are safe here.” The former XO sighs deeply. She glares at her oldest friend for a couple of seconds then sets her eyes on you. She stays silent for a moment, lost in her thoughts. “[pc.name], can I have a pen and paper?” You nod. Without further ado, you search the room for a blank sheet and a pen. After some fumbling and rummaging, you recover a notebook and a pink crayon from a shelf. Paulette gently takes them and starts drawing. At first, she hesitates, but after a few seconds, her fingers start scribbling something on the paper: a sigil, maybe an emblem? “Screams, saws revving, lasers searing my flesh, metal simmering, water bubbling — the feeling of burning while drowning, life leaving my body, one piece at a time — then nothing. Darkness, then a roaring storm, millions of lights snuffed, complexity reduced to simple words, bleakness, obedience, death,” she murmurs, shaking to her core. Her eyes move erratically as her hands scribble a zaika face with long ears, full cheeks, a wicked smile, and big, round glasses. She’s sweating profusely, almost on the verge of tears as she adds two big Xs in the middle of the two white Os. It might be just a black and white logo, but its mere existence gives you the creeps. “An enthusiastic voice, Lithium Flower’s — someone’s berating her. She’s meaner, colder, sharper. I feel her wrath, her disappointment, how she loathes the results. A merciful GU is no use to me! she screams, Start GU-2’s indoctrination!” You put a hand on Paulette’s shoulder. “A-Are you alright?” The zaika startles. She blinks a couple of times then shakes her head. “I’m okay. There’s one more word: E.G.G. - then nothing for a moment. Then, I woke up in Lithium Flower’s room. She’s angry – somebody stole her pet. She wants me to recover it posthaste.” Dabbebieth gasps. “Y-You dreamt of me,” he teases, waving his tentacles around like a happy anemone. Paulette tries to stay stern and serious, but seeing her friend’s silly moves makes her puff out a sigh. “In a way. Everything’s crystal clear: the hunt, boarding the Gloaming, neutralizing the guards, entering the cargo bay, finding your tank, the captain and the half the crew raising their guns, the laser sights on my visor and chest, then everything goes black again.” Wait! Didn’t she say that she remembered everything because the pirates’ EMP grenades fried her inhibitors? Paulette shakes her head. “I thought so... but there’s still a blank in the story. I’m more concerned about your memories. What is the E.G.G. and what is a GU?” “I don’t know,” the former XO replies. “I’m as clueless as you are. Maybe... if we had a lead.” “We’ve got one,” you remark, raising the drawing. “Somebody on Dhaal certainly knows something. This logo is far too iconic to go unnoticed — I’m sure there’s rumors about the E.G.G. or the GUs.” Paulette nods. “I think the E.G.G. was the GUs’ headquarters. I’m sure one of them saw the emblem somewhere.” “Gangers and black market dealers can have good leads too,” Paulette adds. “Trust and favors are very important to them — strength, too. If you want to milk them for data, be ready for a fight.” You nod. “Do you think we should consider Lithium Flower?” The purple zaika grins. “You can try, but I doubt she’ll spill the beans. We’ll do our own investigations. Be careful out there, [pc.name].” “Will do,” you reply. Gangers, artists, illegal establishments, Lithium Flower — this should be fun.

My source narrowly escaped a personnel purge just after the delivery.” Paulette grins. “I have a modular compatibility for insectoid limbs; it wouldn’t surprise me if they decided to make spider XOs out of their workforce. Good job [pc.name].” You smile. “I did my best. She also mentioned a new warden appointed to the building overnight — an XO-smasher, no less. It won’t be a walk in the park.” Dabbebieth floats your way. “This warden gal sounds strong and scary, but this won’t stop us from getting to the bottom of it!” “That’s the spirit,” Paulette adds, patting her friend’s head. “Alone, we are strong, together, we are unstoppable. I’m ready when you are, [pc.name].” ]

original: When you reach Paulette’s room, you find it empty: no zaika, no leyak, only a cozy pillow fort, dozens of exotic plants, and hundreds of chaotically-arranged books. You could have sworn that she was here a moment ago. Your Codex blips. “Looking for me [pc.name]?” “Where are you?” The former XO chuckles. “I’m waiting for you in the meeting room. Don’t be too long or you won’t get your present.” Well, if that isn’t a promise. You turn around and head to the ship’s heart. The halls are suspiciously silent and empty, almost devoid of life. Something evil’s stirring in here. Carefully, you open the door, expecting to be jumped by the ferocious XO. “Surprise!” Dabbebieth{zero plus is crew:, Zero Plus,} and Paulette shout. Your crewmates have prepared a little party for you, how sweet! There’s snacks, drinks, balloons, and a big chocolate cake almost bursting with cream. They really outdid themselves. “What’s this little party for?” Paulette wraps her arms around your waist and places a soft kiss on your cheek. “For everything. In our times of need, you’ve always been here for us. Come, take a seat and enjoy yourself a little. You deserve it.” “Alright,” you reply. “What’s for dinner?” “[zp.isCrew |Zero Plus and Dabbebieth plundered the food stalls |We plundered the food stalls ] to find the best goods on Dhaal. There’s Liashu buns, Firedrake peanuts, Chillkatrice gummies, potato chips, kui-tan nuts, motorblitz, rak’ys liquor, and a cherry-chocolate cake — lactose free of course. I also bought a sweet peach cheesecake but someone ate it before you arrived,” she says, giving the eye to [zp.isCrew |the dollified doctor and Dabbebieth. “The icing looked so shiny and tasty,” she replies, glancing away in shame. “And the jam looked so plump and creamy,” the shy leyak murmurs, cheeks ablaze. |Dabbebieth. “S-Sorry. The jam filling looked so plump and creamy,” the shy leyak murmurs, cheek ablaze. ] It’s not a big deal, there’s still lots of stuff to eat and drink. You can’t wait to dig in. “That’s the spirit. Here, take a seat,” Paulette proposes. Without further ado, you take place at the table and gather some local treats. The motorblitz is strong, especially with Firedrake peanuts, but fortunately, the buns and gummies quickly quench your ardors. Paulette [zp.isCrew |and Zero Plus share some rak’ys together, diluting it with lots of water and syrup before drinking the colorful cocktail. Their cheeks rapidly darken as the alcohol sips into their veins. A few more drinks and they’re likely start making out on top of the table |dilutes it with syrup and lots of water before drinking her colorful cocktail. Her cheeks rapidly darken under the influence of alcohol. Her tail becomes more adventurous too. A few more drinks and she’ll bend you over the table. ] Before the party becomes too intense, you slice the creamy cake and give a piece to everyone. Luckily, the biscuit is soaked in liquorous syrup. The baking steamed the alcohol away, leaving only the fruity flavors to enjoy. “Stars, I adore cherries. The way they pop in your mouth is just divine.” “Dark chocolate’s the best,” Dabbebieth retorts. “I wish we had more on Mome. The planet’s a bit too cold to grow them so they are pretty rare — especially for us leyaks.” [zp.isCrew |“Did you have some before? This stuff’s soooo good!” The little leyak turns cobalt. “I... once stole some when my tribe’s yukkoth flew close to the ground. It took us hours to break the fruits and grind the beans into an edible paste, but it was worth every drop.” |“Didn’t you steal some back on your homeworld?” Paulette asks. The little leyak turns cobalt. “Only a couple of times. It took us hours to break the fruits and grind the beans into an edible paste, but it was worth every drop.” ] You take another bite. Stars, it’s so sweet and light — it’s like tasting a caramel cloud. “[pc.name]?” “Yes Paulette.” “I just... wanted to thank you again. Without you, I’d still be a lost girl stranded on a polluted wasteland. These memories you found for me, they are priceless. Now I know who I am and what I want.” “And what do you want?” you inquire. The former XO blushes. “To make the galaxy a brighter place, for you, for me, for Dabbebieth, for my sisters, for their daughters,{zero plus is crew: for Zero Plus,} and for everyone else!” “That’s very nice of you — and very ambitious. Beware not to go overboard. You don’t need to save the stars alone.” [zp.isCrew |The bimbo nurse nods. “Yeah! We’re here too! Nobody can resist our cutie powers!” Dabbebieth laughs softly. “Count on me. I’ll spread your legend across time and space, one ballad at a time.” |Dabbebieth nods. “You can count on me. I’ll spread your legend across time and space, one ballad at a time.” ] “Not so shy anymore, aren’t you?” The shy leyak pumps his inexistant bust. “That’s what headbutting a mutant chimera and an invincible goo beast does to you,” he proudly boasts. You [pc.mf |chuckle |giggle ]. “Your Grande Finales are impressive, that’s for sure. It was nice teaming up with you, [pc.isAss |kill-stealer |Dabbebieth ].” “The pleasure was all mine,” he impishly replies, bowing down theatrically. His silly demeanor makes Paulette[zp.isCrew]{zero plus is crew: and Zero Plus} laugh. “Dabbebieth, never change.” You enjoy the snacks and drinks, retelling the events fondly, despite how close you were to dying — or to meeting a more sinister end. It feels so nice to see Paulette happy and relaxed again. Her tortured dreams plagued her for far too long. Her body always felt tense, on the edge, as if a monster waited in her shadow, ready to backstab her if she dropped her guard. She’s a completely different zaika now! Paulette raises her glass. “To new horizons!” “To new horizons!” You cheerfully reply[zp.isCrew |, although the bimbo nurse’s reply sounded more like new whorizons ]. The future’s gonna be bright — you’re sure of it.
